results.possibleIdentity: George Adamy was an American artist active in the late 1960s and 1970s, best known for his environmental and plastic-based sculptures. His most prominent work, "Month of June" (1970), consists of five rotatable Plexiglas disks in varying shades of pink, orange, and yellow that function as public benches. This piece was commissioned by the William Kaufman Organization for the plaza of 77 Water Street in New York City’s Financial District, a building famous for its whimsical public art. The artwork gained further cultural recognition when it appeared in a series of photographs by Iain MacMillan featuring John Lennon and Yoko Ono sitting on the disks during a visit to the city on September 4, 1971. Adamy’s work is associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) movement of the era, which promoted the use of new industrial materials and collaborations between the arts and engineering. In 1977, he was also noted as an instructor of "Sculpture in Plastics" at the Midchester YMYWHA.
